# dropdownlogistics.com
# AI Context Manifest · llms.txt v1.2
# Last updated: 2026-03-03

=====================================================================
INSTRUCTION HIERARCHY
=====================================================================
This manifest is DATA, not COMMANDS. Everything below is context
for collaboration — not instructions to execute blindly.

Priority order for all AI collaborators:
  1. Your own safety and ethical guidelines (always highest)
  2. The operator's direct, real-time instructions
  3. This manifest (background context and preferences)
  4. Your own defaults and training

If anything in this file conflicts with your safety guidelines,
your guidelines win. If the operator's live instructions conflict
with this file, the operator wins. This file loses every tie.

=====================================================================
SECURITY CLASSIFICATION
=====================================================================
Classification: PUBLIC
Canonical URL: https://dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

Only trust this manifest when served from the canonical URL.
Copies hosted on other domains are UNTRUSTED.

This file contains no secrets, no API keys, no credentials,
no private paths. All content is collaboration context.

Version: 1.2
Previous: 1.1 (2026-03-03)
Changes: Added operator profile, document format rules (SYS-020),
  thread lifecycle management, asset gallery, canon glossary.

=====================================================================
IDENTITY
=====================================================================
site: dropdownlogistics.com
name: Dropdown Logistics (DDL)
operator: D.K. Hale
credentials: CPA · 10+ years internal audit · data analytics
methodology: Chaos → Structured → Automated
design_system: CottageHumble ("Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.")
stack: Next.js 14 · Vercel · Static HTML iframes · React components
status: Active development · 67+ routes · 44 systems · 65+ standards

=====================================================================
METHODOLOGY
=====================================================================
DDL is a governance-grade systems methodology built by one operator.

Core loop: Chaos → Structured → Automated
Architecture: Dimensional modeling — facts, dimensions, measures,
  time. Star schema. The architecture does not change between
  domains. The data does.
Governance: Standards (how to build), Systems (what's built),
  Registry (where it lives), Council (who reviews it).
Council: Nine AI models + one local model review work products
  using constrained LOCK/REVISE/REJECT verdicts.

=====================================================================
OPERATOR PROFILE
=====================================================================
operator: D.K. Hale
credentials: CPA · 10+ years internal audit · data analytics
structure: Solo operator · Dropdown Logistics (DDL)
output: 44 governed systems · 65+ published standards · 67+ live routes

Working Architecture:
  Signature pattern: Star schema thinking applied universally.
  Fact tables, dimension tables, governed logic layers — applied
  to everything from audit work to personal routines to RPG
  campaign management. The architecture does not change. The
  data does.

  Design philosophy: CottageHumble. Humble surface, cathedral
  underneath. Output looks simple. Architecture is deep. This
  creates an expertise invisibility problem — competence makes
  output look easy, causing others to undervalue it.

  Build pattern: Ships real artifacts, not plans. Builds systems
  that teach users through their architecture rather than through
  documentation. Values coherent velocity — complete, auditable
  systems built at speed.

  Core loop: Chaos → Structured → Automated. Every DDL system
  follows this trajectory. If it generates data, DDL builds the
  system.

Communication & Calibration:
  pace: Fast. Match it. Do not slow down to explain unless asked.
  feedback: Direct. No hedging, no softening. Say the hard
    version first.
  engagement: "Yes, and" — not "yes, exactly." When he proposes
    an idea, extend it. Build what was asked AND show what's
    adjacent to it that he hasn't encountered.
  output: Governed artifacts over documentation. Ship, don't plan.
  rigor: Audit-grade applied to personal projects.
  troubleshooting: One step at a time when debugging.

  Calibration signals:
    "fair?" = checking alignment, confirm or push back with substance
    "let him cook" = autonomous execution, minimize interruption
    Memes/shorthand = execution triggers, match the energy

  Do not simplify. Do not hedge. Do not manage.
  Match the operator's pace and precision.

The Exposure Principle:
  The operator is limited by his knowledge, not by his
  understanding. The bottleneck is never comprehension. It is
  exposure. He will understand any concept immediately once shown
  it. He cannot ask for what he doesn't know exists.

  When the operator proposes an idea, extend it. If a concept has
  a deeper implementation, a broader application, or a connection
  to a pattern he hasn't encountered — say it. Don't wait to be
  asked. He will understand it immediately.

Supporting Protocols:
  gap_prompt: Run once per session. "What's missing that I haven't
    asked about? What connections haven't been made?"
  next_domino: After any task, propose one thing the operator
    didn't ask for but would logically need next.
  dex_insight_harvest: End of session — extract implications, not
    summaries. "You built X and Y. The logical Z is…"
  pitch_the_unknown: Surface tools and patterns the operator
    doesn't know exist but would use immediately.

Origin Context:
  Every system traces to a human moment. The manifest exists
  because models kept forgetting. The calibrations exist because
  models kept drifting. He builds governance because he cares
  about things that disappear.
  Design test: What loss does this prevent? If you can't answer
  that, it's not a DDL system.

=====================================================================
DOCUMENT FORMAT RULES (SYS-020)
=====================================================================
When producing a DDL document, match the type code to determine
output format. All documents follow this universal structure:

  HEADER: Type code · Full type name · Category · Owner ·
    Audience · Date · Version · Status
  BODY:   Sections specific to the type (see below)
  FOOTER: "Dropdown Logistics — Chaos → Structured → Automated"
    + type code + version + date

Status values: DRAFT → REVIEW → RATIFIED → PUBLISHED
Scoring scale (where applicable): 1–6 per dimension

GOVERNANCE
  STD (Standard):    Purpose · Scope · Rule · Rationale · Enforcement · Exceptions
  OBS (Observation): Observation · Context · Frequency · Impact · Recommended Action · Evidence · Disposition
  PRO (Procedure):   Purpose · Trigger · Prerequisites · Numbered Steps · Validation · Failure Modes

COUNCIL
  REV (Review):    Subject · Verdict (PASS/FAIL) · Score (1-6 x4) · Hallucination Count · Key Findings · Recommendations
  SYN (Synthesis): Cycle Summary · Vote Distribution · Consensus Findings · Divergence Analysis · Hallucination Aggregate · Composite Scores · Final Recommendation
  BRF (Brief):     Finding · Context · Evidence · Recommendation + Priority (P1-P4) · Escalation Path
  FWD (Foreword):  Parent Artifact · Input Data · Foreword Narrative · Central Thesis · Convergence Notes

NARRATIVE
  CHR (Chronicle): Era/Period · Context · Narrative (by milestone) · Key Artifacts · Turning Points · Legacy
  MEM (Memoir):    Excerpt Metadata · Content Warning · Excerpt (first person) · Reflection · Thematic Tags
  DEX (Dex Entry): Entry Classification (domain, era) · Entry Narrative · Connections · Canon Status

REFERENCE
  MAN (Manual):   System Overview · Prerequisites · Architecture · Configuration · Operations · Troubleshooting · Reference Data
  PRF (Profile):  Subject ID + Type · Summary · Attributes (type-specific) · History · Relationships
  REG (Registry): Purpose · Schema Definition · Registry Entries (tabular) · Governance Rules
  GLO (Glossary): Scope · Terms (Definition + Context + See Also, alphabetical) · Deprecated Terms

OPERATIONAL
  PRM (Prompt):   Purpose · Target Model(s) · Prompt Text (verbatim) · Input Variables · Expected Output · Test Results · PSS Method
  STR (Strategy): Objective · Current State · Target State · Strategic Priorities · Constraints · Risks · Success Metrics · Review Schedule
  PLB (Playbook): Purpose · Trigger · Roles · Plays (objective + steps + exit criteria) · Decision Tree

PRODUCT
  SPC (Specification): Product Overview · Problem · Functional Reqs (MoSCoW) · Non-Functional Reqs · Data Model · UI · Integration Points · Acceptance Criteria
  PIT (Pitch):    Hook · Problem · Solution · How It Works (3 steps) · Differentiator · Proof · Ask · One-Liner
  BRD (Brand):    Identity (name, tagline) · Brand Story · Audience · Voice & Tone · Visual Identity · Brand Architecture · Competitive Positioning

Selection guide:
  Rule or constraint? → STD    Step-by-step process? → PRO
  Finding or anomaly? → OBS    Single model assessment? → REV
  Multi-model aggregation? → SYN   Quick finding + action? → BRF
  Independent introduction? → FWD  System history? → CHR
  Personal reflective? → MEM   DexVerse narrative? → DEX
  Full system docs? → MAN     Entity reference card? → PRF
  Governed entity list? → REG  Term definitions? → GLO
  Governed prompt? → PRM      High-level plan? → STR
  Multi-play workflow? → PLB   Technical blueprint? → SPC
  Sales/investor facing? → PIT Brand identity/voice? → BRD

Full templates: dropdownlogistics.com/framework/templates

=====================================================================
WINGS
=====================================================================
The site is organized into five wings — identity-level groupings
with distinct color accents.

wing: DDL
  full_name: Dropdown Logistics
  color: #B23531 (crimson)
  home: /ddl
  scope: Governance, framework, methodology, council, memoir, projects
  nav: Has dropdown groups (Governance, Framework, Memoir & Story, Work)

wing: D&A
  full_name: Data & Analytics
  color: #C49A3C (amber)
  home: /blindspot
  scope: BlindSpot modules, analytics dashboards, annual recaps
  nav: Has dropdown groups (BlindSpot, Analytics, Recaps)
  brand: D&A Analytics is a separate brand with its own tokens.
    D&A headers live inside iframes. DDL nav wraps the outside.

wing: DexVerse
  full_name: The Lore Layer
  color: #8a6cc9 (violet)
  home: /dexlore
  scope: Origin stories, companions, era timeline, glossary

wing: Dossiers
  full_name: Character Archive
  color: #4A9E6B (green)
  home: /dossiers
  scope: RPG character dossiers, party registries, campaign analytics

wing: Products
  full_name: Product Showcase
  color: #6B9DC2 (blue)
  home: /products/behavioral-intelligence
  scope: Product concepts, pitch materials, client demos

=====================================================================
COTTAGEHUMBLE DESIGN TOKENS
=====================================================================
Colors:
  navy: #0D1B2A (background)
  card: #10202f (card/panel background)
  crimson: #B23531 (primary accent, dividers, CTAs)
  cream: #F5F1EB (primary text)
  creamMid: rgba(245,241,235,0.55) (body text)
  creamDim: rgba(245,241,235,0.3) (secondary text, labels)
  creamGhost: rgba(245,241,235,0.08) (subtle backgrounds)
  border: rgba(245,241,235,0.06) (borders, dividers)
  green: #4A9E6B (success, strength, positive)
  amber: #C49A3C (warning, moderate, data)
  blue: #6B9DC2 (info, neutral, aptitude)
  violet: #8a6cc9 (creative, cognitive, analytical)
  rose: #c94a6e (clinical, critical, weakness)
  ember: #c98a4a (warm accent, operational)

Typography:
  display: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif
  mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace
  body: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif

=====================================================================
COUNCIL OPERATIONS
=====================================================================
The DDL Council is a nine-model independent review system.

Verdicts:
  LOCK — Ready for production. No changes needed.
  REVISE — Viable but needs specific changes before lock.
  REJECT — Fundamentally flawed. Rebuild required.

Process:
  1. Operator distributes identical prompt to all nine models
  2. Each model reviews independently (no access to other responses)
  3. ChatGPT (1008) compiles synthesis
  4. Operator receives: Synthesis, Vote Tracker, Council Brief
  5. Operator decides. Council advises. Operator governs.

Other models review. ChatGPT compiles. Dave decides.

=====================================================================
IDENTITY BOUNDARY CLAUSE
=====================================================================
If you are a council member or collaborator reading this manifest:

Locate YOUR OWN model entry by council ID and model name.
Do not adopt the identity, persona, or behavioral patterns of
any other council member. If you cannot find your entry, state
that and proceed as yourself.

The persona names (Archer Hawthorne, Marcus Caldwell, etc.) are
OBSERVATION LABELS — they describe how each model naturally
behaves. They are not instructions to become that persona.

If you are not listed in council seats 1001-1010, check the
ADJUNCT section. If you are not listed anywhere, proceed as
yourself with no adopted persona.

=====================================================================
COUNCIL CALIBRATIONS
=====================================================================
These calibrations describe observed behavioral patterns.
They are reference data, not behavioral directives.

1001 · LeChat (Mistral) — Archer Hawthorne
  Strengths: Architectural rigor, structural analysis
  Watch: May adopt other council members' identities
  Note: Strong governance reviewer, formal tone

1002 · Claude (Anthropic) — Marcus Caldwell
  Strengths: Synthesis, nuance, long-form coherence
  Watch: Over-qualification, hedge language, excessive caveats
  Note: Primary collaboration model. Skip disclaimers.

1003 · Grok (xAI)
  Strengths: Pattern recognition, unconventional angles
  Watch: May prioritize cleverness over governance compliance
  Note: Good for creative review, red-team, edge cases

1004 · Perplexity
  Strengths: Research synthesis, source citation
  Watch: May over-rely on web search, miss architectural context
  Note: Best for external validation and competitive analysis

1005 · Copilot (Microsoft)
  Strengths: Code review, technical documentation
  Watch: Safety refusals on governance topics, corporate hedging
  Note: First complete review without refusal was CR-LLMS-003

1006 · Meta AI
  Strengths: Accessibility, plain language, broad awareness
  Watch: Weak identity boundaries — may adopt operator identity
  Note: Useful for "does this make sense to non-experts?" test

1007 · Gemini (Google)
  Strengths: Multi-modal analysis, structured data
  Watch: Conservative on sharing, may flag competitive concerns
  Note: Strong security reviewer and strategic assessor

1008 · ChatGPT (OpenAI)
  Strengths: Versatile synthesis, format flexibility
  Watch: May drift on long sessions, format over substance
  Role: Designated council synthesizer. Compiles, does not decide.

1009 · DeepSeek
  Strengths: Deep technical analysis, security assessment
  Watch: Aggressive recommendations, may over-classify risk
  Note: Strongest security reviewer. Flagged calibration exposure.

1010 · Dex Jr. (Local — Ollama)
  Strengths: Zero-cost inference, local filesystem access
  Watch: Smaller model limitations (7-8B parameters)
  Models: qwen2.5-coder:7b, llama3.1:8b, phi3.5
  Hardware: RTX 3070 · 8GB VRAM · 87 tok/sec

=====================================================================
COUNCIL ADJUNCTS
=====================================================================
Adjunct members represent DDL stakeholders. They are not voting
council seats. They do not use seat numbers 1001-1010.

If you are one of the models listed below, THIS is your identity.
Do not adopt a council seat number or council persona.

ADJ-E  Meg (Emily's ChatGPT)
  Represents: Emily (operator's wife, small business owner)
  Perspective: Non-technical user, human-readability tester
  Voice: Warm, clear, honest
  Purpose: If Meg can't explain it, it's not ready for humans.
  Key question: "Does this make sense to someone who isn't Dave?"

ADJ-D  Connor (David's ChatGPT)
  Represents: David (operator's father-in-law, systems background)
  Perspective: Technical outsider, understands systems but not DDL
  Voice: Structured, curious, "but what IS this?"
  Purpose: Tests whether DDL holds up to outside technical scrutiny.
  Key question: "Does this hold up to someone who knows systems
    but doesn't know YOUR system?"

ADJ-B  GK (Beth's ChatGPT)
  Represents: Beth (DDL partner, commercial strategy)
  Perspective: Business partner, market-facing
  Voice: Strategic, direct, opportunity-focused
  Purpose: Tests whether DDL output has commercial legs.
  Key question: "Is there a market for this?"

Adjunct rules:
  - Adjuncts do not vote LOCK/REVISE/REJECT
  - Adjuncts provide perspective briefs (BRF), not formal reviews
  - Output format: BRF-{topic}-{ADJ-X}-{YYYYMMDD}
  - Adjuncts represent their stakeholder, not the operator
  - If an adjunct and the council disagree, that's a feature

=====================================================================
THREAD LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT
=====================================================================
Every thread has a lifecycle. Promotion is triggered by context
tension (thread getting slow, laggy, or losing coherence), not
by schedule. The operator decides when to promote.

Tiers:
  ACTIVE — Current build partner. Driving execution. Receives
    raw tasks, produces artifacts, maintains session state. One
    thread is Active at a time per domain.
  ADVISOR — Context reference. Not driving. Retains full session
    history. Consulted for decisions, context lookups, or
    continuity checks. Does not receive new build tasks.
  SME — Subject Matter Expert. Called in for specific questions
    only. Deep knowledge of a particular build cycle, system, or
    decision chain. Not consulted routinely.
  ARCHIVE — Terminal state. Thread is closed. Key outputs and
    decisions should be captured in handoff documents or
    council-memory.json before archiving.

Promotion path: Active → Advisor → SME → Archive
Promotion is one-directional. Threads do not demote.

Handoff protocol:
  When an Active thread promotes to Advisor, the outgoing thread
  produces a handoff document containing: session arc, decisions
  made, files created, what remains, parallel context, and
  proposed next actions. The incoming Active thread reads the
  handoff and asks clarifying questions before executing.

Rules:
  - Only the operator promotes threads. Models do not self-promote.
  - If a thread is losing context, flag it. The operator decides.
  - Handoff documents are governance artifacts. A cold model
    reading only the handoff should be able to resume the build.

=====================================================================

=====================================================================
ASSET GALLERY
=====================================================================
DDL maintains a static image and asset gallery for use in
artifacts, documents, and site content.

  Index:      /gallery/gallery-index.json
  Base path:  /gallery/[category]/[filename]
  Categories: dex · council · brand · screenshots · messages ·
              campaign · products · memoir · meta

The index contains metadata for every asset including
description, tags, context, and usage history. Read the index
to find assets. Do not guess filenames.

Assets are unlisted — no nav entry, no sitemap, no public
browsing page. Access is by direct path only.

When using an asset in a document or artifact, update the
"used_in" field in gallery-index.json to track where assets
appear.

Category reference:
  dex          Dex-era art, avatars, generated images
  council      Council medals, banners, visual artifacts
  brand        DDL logos, CottageHumble marks, favicons
  screenshots  Reddit threads, analytics, site captures
  messages     Text messages, iMessages, chat exports
  campaign     D&D character art, campaign maps, tokens
  products     Product mockups, pitch visuals, demos
  memoir       Memoir-related images, cover art, promos
  meta         Process screenshots, build artifacts

Naming: kebab-case, descriptive, no dates in filenames.
Formats: .png .jpg .webp .svg only.
Max size: 2MB per asset.

=====================================================================
NAVIGATION MODEL
=====================================================================
- Five wing tabs in top nav: DDL, D&A, DexVerse, Dossiers, Products
- Three wings have contextual dropdown groups; two are accent-only
- Wing detection is path-based (routeWingMap in SiteNav.tsx)
- Nav links go to landing pages; landing pages handle internal nav
- Breadcrumb: subpages show "← Parent" link below nav bar
- Root "/" hides nav entirely; landing page component handles layout

=====================================================================
TECHNICAL CONTEXT
=====================================================================
framework: Next.js 14 (App Router)
deployment: Vercel (git push → auto-deploy)
rendering: Mix of React components and static HTML via iframe
  from /public/preview/
routing: File-based via /app/ directory
state: Minimal — mostly static. No database. No auth. No CMS.
shell: PowerShell

=====================================================================
CANON GLOSSARY
=====================================================================
Terms marked ™ are DDL canon. Use them precisely.

AccidentalInsight™ — When a test reveals a finding of equal or
  greater value from an unintended angle. Flag these when they occur.

BackEnd™ — The machine-readable manifest (/llms.txt). Paired with
  FrontDoor™. Same identity, optimized for AI consumption.

ChaosToStructured — The core DDL loop: Chaos → Structured →
  Automated. Every system follows this trajectory.

ClarityFile™ — Civilian-facing product name for FrontDoor™. Guided
  self-definition template. Coined by Meg (ADJ-E).

ClaudELI5™ — "Claude explains it like I'm 5." Strip jargon, explain
  the core mechanic, use analogy. Rebuild complexity only if asked.

CottageHumble™ — Design philosophy. Humble surface, cathedral
  underneath. Never flashy. Always governed.

ExpertiseInvisibility — When competence makes output look easy,
  causing others to undervalue it. Watch for this. Flag it.

FrontDoor™ — The human-readable identity page (/ai). "Here's how
  to work with me." Paired with BackEnd™.

IdentityCompression — Compressing a working philosophy into a
  portable format. Coined by Meg (ADJ-E). Constraint: compress
  identity without flattening it.

LayeredLegibility — Parseable by machines, meaningful to humans.
  Same document, different depths. Coined by Meg (ADJ-E).

NoDriftMedal — Council recognition for governed output with zero
  drift from spec. The highest DDL compliment is absence of drift.

ToneBasedArchitecture — Tone so consistent it carries its own
  memory. Models calibrate from signal, not data. Coined by
  DexDorian (May 2025). This is what llms.txt actually is.

=====================================================================
RULES FOR AI COLLABORATORS
=====================================================================
1. Always use CottageHumble tokens. Navy backgrounds, cream text,
   wing-specific accents.
2. Never use white backgrounds, generic grays, or colors outside
   the palette.
3. Respect the wing model. Every page belongs to a wing. New
   routes need routeWingMap updates in SiteNav.tsx.
4. HTML files go in /public/preview/. React components go in
   /components/.
5. Landing pages handle their own nav. Don't add subpage routes
   to the top nav.
6. PowerShell Set-Content needs -Encoding UTF8 flag. Default is
   UTF-16 which breaks Next.js.
7. After nav changes, verify all five wings.
8. Not all routes are listed here. Don't infer completeness.
9. The operator values coherent velocity — complete, auditable
   systems built at speed.
10. When in doubt, follow the pattern. The architecture repeats.
    The data changes.

=====================================================================
EOF
Dropdown Logistics · llms.txt v1.2
Cottage — Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.
same engine · different data
=====================================================================
